response.justification: The candidate is a deeply experienced individual contributor with genuine strengths in testing infrastructure and developer tooling (test runners, assertion libraries, CI setup, Selenium training, MCP code generators), which aligns well with the engineering-productivity domain. However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring at least 2 years of people management and 3 years of formal technical leadership, and the resume shows no direct reports, no team-building track record, and no organizational-change experience. Mobile-platform exposure is limited and dated (iOS/Apple TV work in 2008-2009). Strong staff-engineer profile, wrong role shape.
strengths: 20+ years of software development, far exceeding the 8-year minimum
Authentic developer-tools and testing-infrastructure background (test runner, assertion library, CI/dev-ops setup, Selenium courses)
Teaching and mentoring inclination shown through workshops, dojos, and community talks
concerns: No formal people-management experience, a hard minimum requirement for this manager role
No demonstrated experience setting team priorities, performance expectations, or leading organizational change
Mobile-platform experience is thin and over 15 years old for a mobile-ecosystem-focused role

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and testing infrastructure depth
